% =========================================================================%
% Table 1: Main Results - Model Performance Comparison
% =========================================================================%

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Performance comparison on dopaminergic differentiation trajectory prediction. All models evaluated on identical test set (n=30 trajectories) with normalized features [0,1]. MAE: Mean Absolute Error.}
\label{tab:model_comparison}
\begin{tabular}{lccc}
\toprule
\textbf{Model} & \textbf{Test MAE} & \textbf{Improvement} & \textbf{Parameters} \\
\midrule
Linear Regression & 0.0998 & baseline & 8 \\
Random Forest & 0.1017 & 1.8\% & 100 trees \\
\textbf{Transformer} & \textbf{0.0805} & \textbf{+19.4\%} & 827K \\
Physics-only (ODE) & 0.4334 & 334.1\% & 4 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% =========================================================================%
% Table 2: Ablation Study - Detailed Breakdown
% =========================================================================%

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Ablation study showing per-feature prediction errors. P: pluripotency score, D: differentiation score. All models trained on D11$\rightarrow$D30 transitions, tested on held-out D30$\rightarrow$D52 transitions.}
\label{tab:ablation_study}
\begin{tabular}{lcccc}
\toprule
\textbf{Model} & \textbf{MAE (Overall)} & \textbf{MAE (P)} & \textbf{MAE (D)} & \textbf{Train Time} \\
\midrule
Linear Regression & 0.0998 & 0.1023 & 0.0974 & <1 min \\
Random Forest & 0.1017 & 0.1036 & 0.0997 & 2 min \\
\textbf{Transformer} & \textbf{0.0805} & \textbf{0.0848} & \textbf{0.0762} & \textbf{15 min} \\
Physics-only (ODE) & 0.4334 & 0.7039 & 0.1629 & <1 min \\
\bottomrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\footnotesize Note: ODE performs well on D predictions (0.1629) but poorly on P predictions (0.7039).} \\
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% =========================================================================%
% Table 3: Temporal Extrapolation Results
% =========================================================================%

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Temporal generalization performance. Model trained on early-stage differentiation (D11$\rightarrow$D30) and tested on late-stage extrapolation (D30$\rightarrow$D52). Negative degradation indicates improvement.}
\label{tab:temporal_extrapolation}
\begin{tabular}{lccc}
\toprule
\textbf{Stage} & \textbf{Transition} & \textbf{MAE} & \textbf{Change} \\
\midrule
Validation (Early) & D11 $\rightarrow$ D30 & 0.1168 & baseline \\
Test (Late) & D30 $\rightarrow$ D52 & 0.0976 & \textbf{-16.4\%} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Per-feature (Early):}} \\
\quad Pluripotency & & 0.0975 & \\
\quad Differentiation & & 0.1361 & \\
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Per-feature (Late):}} \\
\quad Pluripotency & & 0.1018 & 4.3\% \\
\quad Differentiation & & 0.0934 & \textbf{-31.4\%} \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% =========================================================================%
% Table 4: Lambda Network Analysis
% =========================================================================%

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Learned weighting parameter $\lambda$ from hybrid digital twin. $\lambda \approx 0$ indicates ML model dominance, $\lambda \approx 1$ indicates physics dominance. All statistics computed on test set (n=20 trajectories, 60 states).}
\label{tab:lambda_analysis}
\begin{tabular}{lc}
\toprule
\textbf{Statistic} & \textbf{Value} \\
\midrule
Mean & $9.8e-08$ \\
Std. Dev & $9.5e-08$ \\
Median & $5.9e-08$ \\
Min & $7.5e-09$ \\
Max & $2.9e-07$ \\
\midrule
Validation Loss & 0.0766 \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Correlation with state:}} \\
\quad $\lambda$ vs. P & -0.655 \\
\quad $\lambda$ vs. D & -0.367 \\
\bottomrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\footnotesize Interpretation: $\lambda \approx 0$ with validation loss 0.0766} \\
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\footnotesize indicates ML model dominance (consistent with Transformer's 0.0805 MAE).} \\
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% =========================================================================%
% Table 5: Compact Summary Table
% =========================================================================%

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Summary of key results. Transformer achieves best overall performance (+19.4\% improvement), while $\lambda \approx 0$ indicates ML model dominance in hybrid architecture.}
\label{tab:summary}
\begin{tabular}{lcc}
\toprule
\textbf{Metric} & \textbf{Value} & \textbf{Comparison} \\
\midrule
Best Baseline MAE & 0.0998 & Linear Regression \\
Transformer MAE & 0.0805 & +19.4\% improvement \\
\midrule
Temporal Extrapolation & -16.4\% & Better on late stage \\
\midrule
$\lambda$ (Hybrid Weight) & $\approx 10^{-7}$ & ML dominance \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% =========================================================================%
% Table 6: Computational Efficiency (Inference Latency & Memory)
% =========================================================================%

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Computational efficiency comparison. Inference latency measured as median over 100 runs on CPU (win32). Memory footprint measured via serialized model size.}
\label{tab:computational_efficiency}
\begin{tabular}{lcccc}
\toprule
\textbf{Model} & \textbf{Latency (ms)} & \textbf{Batch (ms)} & \textbf{Memory (MB)} & \textbf{Parameters} \\
\midrule
Linear Regression & 0.055 & 0.057 & 0.0005 & 10 \\
Random Forest & 27.857 & 28.512 & 0.5348 & 6,704 \\
\textbf{Transformer} & \textbf{1.625} & \textbf{2.310} & \textbf{3.6624} & \textbf{827,010} \\
Physics-only (ODE) & 2.125 & 112.396 & 0.0021 & 6 \\
\bottomrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\footnotesize Latency: single sample; Batch: 30 test samples. All measurements on CPU.} \\
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
